name: Aniruddha
description: Aniruddha (Sanskrit: अनिरुद्ध, lit. 'unconquerable')[2] is a character in Hindu mythology, the son of Pradyumna and Rukmavati, and the grandson of Krishna and Rukmini.[3] He is said to have been very much like his grandfather, to the extent that he is considered by some to be a Jana avatar, an avatar of Vishnu. He is a member of the chatur-vyuha, the four Vrishni heroes.

infobox.Texts: Bhagavata Purana, Vishnu Purana, Shiva Purana, Mahabharata
